zh.Congham, Kings Lynn and West Norfolk Bor: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可從哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd於1320年出生在英國諾福爾可從哈姆時，他的父親Geoffrey Le Norreys30歲，母親Lady Amabilla Decleseby30歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1382年在他的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可從哈姆。

zh-hans.Congham, Kings Lynn and West Norfolk Bor: 英国英格兰諾福爾可從哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd于1320年出生在英国諾福爾可從哈姆时，他的父亲Geoffrey Le Norreys30岁，母亲Lady Amabilla Decleseby30岁。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1382年在他的家乡享年62岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可從哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Norreys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Norris
meanings: habitational name from Middle English norreis, noreis, norais ‘northerner’ (Anglo-Norman French noreis), used both of Norwegians and of inhabitants of northern England and Scotland.
from the Middle English personal name Norreis, derived from norreis ‘northerner’.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd was born in 1320, in Congham, Norfolk, England, his father, Geoffrey Le Norreys, was 30 and his mother, Lady Amabilla Decleseby, was 30. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1382, in his hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in Congham, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd est né en 1320, à Congham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Geoffrey Le Norreys, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Amabilla Decleseby, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1382, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Congham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd 1320 in Congham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffrey Le Norreys 30 und seine Mutter Lady Amabilla Decleseby 30. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1382 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Congham, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd è nato il nel 1320 nel Congham, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Geoffrey Le Norreys, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lady Amabilla Decleseby, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1382, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Congham, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey le Norreys 2ndが1320年にイギリスノーフォルクコンガムで生まれた時，父Geoffrey Le Norreysは30歳，母Lady Amabilla Declesebyは30歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1382年に故郷で62歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクコンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd(이)가 1320년 영국 노르폴크 콘그함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey Le Norreys(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Amabilla Decleseby(은)는 30세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1382년 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 콘그함에 묻혔다.

mn.Congham, Kings Lynn and West Norfolk Bor: Congham, Norðfolc, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd nasceu em 1320, em Congham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Geoffrey Le Norreys, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Amabilla Decleseby, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1382, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Congham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd родился в 1320 г. в местности конгхам, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Geoffrey Le Norreys было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Amabilla Decleseby, 30. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1382 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности конгхам, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Geoffrey le Norreys 2nd nació en 1320, en Congham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Geoffrey Le Norreys, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady Amabilla Decleseby, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1382, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Congham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
